name: Col de Pouech – loop starting from Pla de la Lau – Mont Valier national reserve – Pyrénées Ariégeoises regional natural park
name_original: Col de Pouech – boucle au départ du Pla de la Lau – Réserve domaniale du mont Valier – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises
region_intro: Hiking trails around Les Bordes-Sur-Lez are situated within the Ariège Pyrenees, offering a diverse landscape for outdoor activities. The region is characterized by high peaks, extensive natural woodlands, and several mountain lakes and tarns. Trails often traverse varied terrain, from lower-altitude forests to higher mountain pastures and historical sites. Best hiking trails around Les Bordes-Sur-Lez The most popular hiking route is Col de Pouech – loop starting from Pla de la Lau – Mont Valier national reserve – Pyrénées Ariégeoises regional natural park, a 10.8 miles (17.4 km) trail that takes 8 hours 53 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain within the Mont Valier national reserve. Another top favourite among local hikers is Les Estagnous Refuge – Cascade de Nerech loop from Bordes-Uchentein, a difficult 9.1 miles (14.6 km) path. This trail leads to the Les Estagnous Refuge and passes the Cascade de Nerech waterfall. Local hikers also love the Nérech Waterfall – Cascade de Nerech loop from Uchentein d’En Haut, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 19 minutes. Hiking around Les Bordes-Sur-Lez is defined by Pyrenean mountain ranges, mountain lakes, and extensive forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and difficult ascents. The routes in Les Bordes-Sur-Lez are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 450 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Les Bordes-Sur-Lez's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking trails can I expect around Les Bordes-Sur-Lez?
A: The Les Bordes-Sur-Lez area, nestled in the Ariège Pyrenees, offers a diverse range of hiking trails. You'll find routes traversing high peaks, extensive natural woodlands, and beautiful mountain lakes. The trails vary in difficulty, with 4 moderate and 7 difficult routes available, catering to different experience levels.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, several circular routes allow you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956905331/col-de-pouech-loop-starting-from-pla-de-la-lau-mont-valier-national-reserve-pyrenees-ariegeoises-regional-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Pouech – loop starting from Pla de la Lau&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 10.8-mile loop through the Mont Valier national reserve. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31521281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Estagnous Refuge – Cascade de Nerech loop from Bordes-Uchentein&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 9.1-mile trail.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore stunning Pyrenean landscapes, including high peaks and mountain tarns. Notable lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2483486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Bethmale&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/673275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ayes Pond&lt;/a&gt; are popular destinations. You might also encounter waterfalls such as the Cascade d'Aubert, and historic 'bourdaous' – mountain pastoral hamlets. The area also features several mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3104691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Pouech&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls?
A: Absolutely! The area is known for its beautiful waterfalls. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35304813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nérech Waterfall – Cascade de Nerech loop from Uchentein d’En Haut&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.8-mile trail that takes you directly to the Cascade de Nerech. Another challenging route that passes this waterfall is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31565765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascade de Nerech – Nérech Waterfall loop from Bordes-Uchentein&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Les Bordes-Sur-Lez?
A: The Ariège Pyrenees are generally best for hiking from late spring through early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is typically more stable, and higher mountain trails are free of snow. Summer offers lush landscapes, while autumn brings vibrant colors. Always check local weather conditions before heading out, especially for higher elevations.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Les Bordes-Sur-Lez?
A: The hiking trails around Les Bordes-Sur-Lez are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 450 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning Pyrenean views, the tranquility of the mountain lakes, and the diverse terrain that offers both challenging ascents and peaceful forest walks.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest along the trails?
A: Yes, the region has a rich history. Some trails offer a glimpse into the area's past, such as routes exploring the 'bourdaous' – traditional mountain pastoral hamlets. For those interested in industrial heritage, trails like 'Les Mines de Bentaillou' from Eylie (not a komoot route in this guide, but mentioned in region research) offer a chance to discover old mining sites with ruins and equipment.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options in the area?
A: Yes, the GR 10, a renowned long-distance hiking trail that crosses the Ariège Pyrenees, passes through the broader region. While specific segments are not detailed in this guide, its presence indicates opportunities for multi-day treks and connecting with a wider network of trails for experienced hikers.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on hikes in this region?
A: Given its location in the Pyrenees, hikes around Les Bordes-Sur-Lez often feature significant elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956905331/col-de-pouech-loop-starting-from-pla-de-la-lau-mont-valier-national-reserve-pyrenees-ariegeoises-regional-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Pouech loop&lt;/a&gt; involves over 1500 meters of ascent, making it a challenging endeavor. Even moderate routes can include several hundred meters of climbing, so be prepared for varied terrain.

Q: Are there any refuges or accommodation options along the trails?
A: While specific accommodation details are not provided for all routes, the region does feature mountain refuges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31521281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Estagnous Refuge&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example, accessible via a challenging loop from Bordes-Uchentein. The Montgarri Refuge and Sanctuary, located in the nearby Aran Valley, also offers accommodation and services for hikers in a unique natural setting.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in total around Les Bordes-Sur-Lez?
A: There are 11 hiking routes detailed in this guide for the Les Bordes-Sur-Lez area. These include a mix of moderate and difficult trails, offering various distances and elevation profiles to explore the stunning Pyrenean landscape.
tips: Here is a lodge and restaurant located by the stream and in the middle of the mountains. You can bivouac around to sleep in the middle of nature.

The Nerech waterfall is one of the most powerful in the Pyrenees. It flows into the heart of Couserans (vast territory combining high mountains, mountains and foothills, and covered areas).
It is home to rich biodiversity. Among the species most observed here, there is a very wide variety of wild orchids.
Waterfall just above the Cascade de Nerech.

Here we find a refuge where we can spend the night. You can also pitch your tent nearby. It is located a few steps from a lake and you can admire the Rond pond in the distance. The views are breathtaking!
The Col de Pouech offers breathtaking views of the surrounding mountains. You can also appreciate the Round and Long ponds in the distance.
A moraine is a mass of blocks and rock debris detached from a glacier. Hence this unusual landscape with rocks which bear the traces of this phenomenon.
This pond is at the foot of the Col de la Crouzette. The setting is exceptional, so take the opportunity to take pictures!
